description_long: This object is a large, vintage-style modular synthesizer control cabinet, featuring a wooden frame and a black front panel densely populated with dials, switches, patch cables, and a keyboard. The upper section is filled with a complex array of knobs, jacks, and colored cables, suggesting a highly customizable electronic interface. A handwritten note is pinned to the top left, adding a touch of realism and human interaction. The lower section houses a full-sized keyboard, while the sides and back are made of plain wood and black paneling, giving it a sturdy, boxy appearance. The cabinet stands on four legs, with an open space beneath the keyboard, typical of floor-standing electronic equipment. The overall design is reminiscent of classic analog synthesizer workstations used in music production and sound engineering.
description: A large, vintage modular synthesizer control cabinet with a wooden frame, black panel, numerous dials, patch cables, and a keyboard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full array of dials, patch cables, a keyboard, and a handwritten note pinned to the top left.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wooden side panel and the depth of the cabinet, with some cables visible protruding from the front.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain black and wooden back panel, with no visible controls or cables.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the wooden side and the protruding front section with cables.
